title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: US and Israeli forces have conducted extensive airstrikes on Iranian military infrastructure since late February 2026, degrading missile stockpiles, air defenses, and naval assets amid escalating tensions over the Strait of Hormuz blockade. In the past week, US special operations teams executed combat search-and-rescue missions inside Iran on April 4, successfully extracting downed F-15 pilots—the first confirmed US ground insertions—prompting debates on whether these qualify as market resolution. Thousands of additional troops, including Marines and the 82nd Airborne, have deployed to regional bases, fueling speculation of limited raids on islands or nuclear sites rather than full invasion. President Trump issued April 5 ultimatums threatening strikes on power plants and bridges if Iran rejects Hormuz concessions, with ceasefire talks stalled and Iranian missile attacks on Israel continuing as of April 6. Traders monitor for procedural definitions of "entry" and next diplomatic deadlines.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain tops trader consensus at 16% implied probability for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, buoyed by their flawless UEFA qualifying campaign and Euro 2024 triumph with stars like Lamine Yamal peaking, but France (14%), England (11%), defending champions Argentina (9%), and Brazil (9%) remain tightly bunched amid the recent group stage draw and playoff resolutions. March 26-31 qualifiers saw Czechia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, and Türkiye secure spots via shootouts and narrow wins, underscoring European depth, while CONMEBOL's tight table with Argentina leading and Brazil fifth highlights South American parity. Mounting injuries—Rodrygo sidelined for Brazil, Mbappé nursing an ankle sprain, Foden and Raphinha concerns—further balance the field in the expanded 48-team format, where manageable groups foster multiple knockout paths without clear superpowers.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential primary field following the party's 2024 setbacks, California Governor Gavin Newsom commands trader consensus at 24.1% implied probability on Polymarket, propelled by a March UC Berkeley-POLITICO poll showing him leading Kamala Harris 28%-14% among California Democratic primary leaners—widening his home-state edge amid his national book tour and anti-Trump events like SXSW appearances. National surveys remain mixed, with Harris topping some at 21-31% but traders discount her at 4.3% due to post-election fatigue. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 8.3% draws progressive support, while Jon Ossoff's 6.6% reflects Georgia battleground appeal. Consolidation hinges on 2026 midterm results for governors like Shapiro and Whitmer, Q2 fundraising disclosures, and early endorsements from party leaders.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal mediated by regional actors like Pakistan's Asim Munir and received on April 6, which called for immediate de-escalation, an end to hostilities, and reopening the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian missile sites and IRGC facilities. President Trump's looming deadline for Iranian capitulation, coupled with threats of infrastructure strikes, underscores stalled diplomacy following failed 2025-2026 negotiations that sparked open conflict after Israel's preemptive attacks. Tehran views the temporary truce as effective surrender, demanding permanent terms, while mediators push phased deals. Traders assess fragile talks against persistent military operations and escalation signals as key resolution drivers.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler tops trader consensus at 13.5% implied probability for the green jacket, buoyed by his world No. 1 ball-striking prowess and strong Augusta National course history including two prior wins, despite withdrawing from the Houston Open for his second child's birth and addressing an early-season form dip. Jon Rahm (7.8%) and Bryson DeChambeau (6.5%) trail closely on LIV Golf heater—Rahm's top-five streak all 2026 and elite greens in regulation rate, DeChambeau's back-to-back victories capped by last week's playoff win over Rahm. Defending champion Rory McIlroy (6.5%), fresh off his 2025 career Grand Slam clincher, faces back injury questions post-Players Championship, while Xander Schauffele and Ludvig Aberg round a bunched top-five amid Augusta's unforgiving layout, weather-neutral forecast, and parity between PGA Tour and LIV form keeping the 95-player field wide open.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance edges California Governor Gavin Newsom as trader consensus frontrunner on Polymarket for the 2028 presidential election winner, with Florida Senator Marco Rubio third in a wide-open field reflecting high uncertainty over two years out. Recent CPAC and New Hampshire straw polls showing Rubio surging past 30% among Republicans have boosted his odds to 10%, while head-to-head matchups favor Newsom over Vance, narrowing the gap amid Democratic primary jockeying. Vance's vice-presidential incumbency provides a path-to-victory edge via party machinery, but lacks President Trump's explicit endorsement. Dynamics remain tight due to fluid GOP succession dynamics and untested national turnout; 2026 midterms, official announcements, economic trends, or scandals could create separation by solidifying nominations or shifting battleground state polls.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the frontrunner at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, driven by his high-profile role as HHS Secretary, alignment with President Trump's agenda, and the growing MAHA movement's appeal to the GOP base amid midterm campaigning, despite some Senate Republican pushback on his health policy initiatives. Vice President J.D. Vance holds 37.6%, buoyed by his position as heir apparent but tempered by recent odds dips below prior highs, uncertainty over his 2028 intentions amid Iran policy tensions, and falling below 37% in late March. Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 21.8% gains from a CPAC straw poll surge to 35%, Trump praise, and foreign policy visibility, with 2026 midterms looming as a key test for early primary positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 39.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions, bolstered by their league-best 62-16 record and clinched No. 1 Western Conference seed, securing home-court advantage through the playoffs starting April 18. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play and the young core's depth have dominated the late regular season, extending a strong finish after entering the final stretch at 60-16. San Antonio Spurs follow at 16.5%, riding Victor Wembanyama's dominance and a near-11-game win streak snapped recently, positioning them as No. 2 West seed at 59-19 with scouts highlighting their postseason matchup edge. Boston Celtics (11.9%) and Denver Nuggets (8.5%) trail amid tougher Eastern paths and injury concerns, while Detroit Pistons (5.5%) surge in the East but face steeper odds against West powers.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen top trader consensus at 37.4% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, propelled by their landslide UMK victory in late February with the violin-driven "Liekinheitin," which has dominated fan polls, projections like The Model (leading by 36 points), and betting markets since. Strong jury-televote balance and viral stage presence have sustained momentum amid recent first semi-final running order reveals positioning them favorably. Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund (10.5%) follows after a solid Dansk Melodi Grand Prix win with "Før Vi Går Hjem," while France's Monroe (10.3%) and Greece's techno-infused "Ferto" by Akylas hold jury appeal. With semis on May 12/14 and final May 16, rehearsals and previews could shift the closely watched Nordic-Greek-French rivalry.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 7?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $58,000 on April 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: Elon Musk # tweets March 31 - April 7, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 31 12:00 PM ET to April 7, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Elon Musk posting 280-299 times from March 31 to April 7, 2026, at 62.5% implied probability, reflecting his sustained late-March cadence of around 37 posts per day as seen in resolved markets like March 27-April 3 and the full month's 1,320-1,359 total averaging over 42 daily. The prior week's lower 220-239 count for March 24-31 introduced some caution, boosting 260-279 to 18%, yet viral engagement on X—spanning AI advancements like Grok Imagine, political culture wars, and wokeness critiques—signals a rebound. With the period ending at 12:00 PM ET today, Musk's morning activity could solidify the frontrunner or spark an upset into 300+.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Hungary's parliamentary election five days away on April 12, trader consensus prices Péter Magyar as the strong favorite at 65.5% implied probability to become next Prime Minister, driven by Tisza party's consistent poll leads over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz—including Medián's March survey showing Tisza at 58% among decided voters versus Fidesz's 35%, a 23-point gap widened from February. Europe Elects' polling average recently had Tisza at 49% to Fidesz's 41%. Orbán retains incumbency advantages, rural strongholds, and an electoral system favoring larger parties, but Magyar's surge with young urban voters amid economic pressures and governance critiques sustains his edge, while minor candidates trail far behind.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' George Russell leads trader consensus at 43.5% implied probability for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship, edging teammate Kimi Antonelli at 31.2%, reflecting the Silver Arrows' total dominance in the opening three Grands Prix—Aussie, Chinese, and Japanese—where they locked out 1-2 finishes and hold a 45-point constructors' lead. Antonelli's back-to-back victories in China and Japan, capped by a commanding Suzuka win under safety car conditions, made him the youngest-ever championship leader at 72 points to Russell's 63, but traders favor the Briton's racecraft and experience amid Toto Wolff's green light for intra-team rivalry sans contact. Ferrari's Leclerc (5.9%) and Hamilton trail due to pace deficits, while Red Bull's Verstappen and McLaren duo languish from reliability woes and qualifying struggles.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 98% implied probability of no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting strong consensus after the March 17-18 decision to hold steady at 3.50%-3.75% amid resilient economic conditions. The March nonfarm payrolls report, released April 3, showed 178,000 jobs added—exceeding forecasts—and unemployment ticking down to 4.3%, bolstering the Fed's view of a solid labor market not yet warranting easing despite moderating inflation. Geopolitical tensions and oil price spikes have further delayed rate-cut expectations into later 2026. Realistic challenges include a surprise downturn in upcoming March CPI data (due mid-April) or weakening indicators that could prompt a dovish pivot, though trader sentiment sees minimal risk pre-meeting.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil front-month futures have surged above $115 per barrel, propelled by supply disruptions from escalating US-Iran tensions and a reported war impacting exports, creating a record US oil premium over global benchmarks. The EIA's latest Weekly Petroleum Status Report for the week ending March 27 showed a 5.5 million barrel build in US commercial crude inventories to 461.6 million barrels—slightly above the five-year average—while Cushing stocks continue multi-week draws, supporting near-term tightness. OPEC+ members met April 5 to discuss potential output hikes of 206,000 barrels per day for April amid stability concerns. Traders eye this week's EIA report and IEA Oil Market Report for demand signals before month-end resolution, with the backwardated futures curve implying persistent supply risks versus softer later-2026 pricing.

title: Blackhawks vs. Sharks
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 6 at 10:00PM ET:
If the Blackhawks win, the market will resolve to "Blackhawks".
If the Sharks win, the market will resolve to "Sharks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: San Jose Sharks host the Chicago Blackhawks in a late-season Pacific Division clash, with the Sharks (36-32-7, 79 points) clinging to faint wild card hopes two points behind Nashville, while the Blackhawks (28-35-14, 70 points) focus on development after snapping a five-game skid with a 4-2 win over Seattle. Key injuries sideline Blackhawks defensemen Matt Grzelcyk (undisclosed) and Artyom Levshunov (hand), though forward Andrew Mangiapane returns after nine games out; Sharks miss winger Ryan Reaves (upper body). Spencer Knight (2.71 GAA) starts for Chicago against Alex Nedeljkovic (2.91 GAA) for San Jose at SAP Center, spotlighting Connor Bedard (70 points) versus rookie Macklin Celebrini (106 points). Chicago won their February head-to-head 6-3, but home ice and motivation favor the Sharks' trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the tight Western Conference wild-card race, the Los Angeles Kings reclaimed the No. 2 spot with a 3-2 shootout win over the Nashville Predators on April 6 at Crypto.com Arena, avenging Nashville's 5-4 shootout victory four days prior and highlighting both teams' strong goaltending from Juuse Saros (26 saves) and Darcy Kuemper. Steven Stamkos extended his goal streak to three games for the Preds, while Adrian Kempe clinched it for LA. Nashville's Nic Hague remains day-to-day with an upper-body injury; Kings are without Kevin Fiala (season-ending leg), Andrei Kuzmenko (meniscus), and Alex Turcotte. Head-to-head tilts emphasize power-play efficiency and late-game discipline amid playoff positioning pressures and schedule fatigue.

title: Iran military action against ___ by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on a listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of a listed country on the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will not be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the escalating US-Israel-Iran conflict that erupted on February 28, 2026, with joint US and Israeli airstrikes targeting Iranian nuclear sites, military infrastructure, and leadership in Tehran, Iran retaliated immediately on March 1 by launching missile and drone strikes on US bases in UAE, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and other Gulf hosts, alongside attacks on Israeli targets including Ben Gurion Airport, Tel Aviv, and Haifa. Tit-for-tat escalations persisted through March, featuring Iranian ballistic missiles with cluster warheads hitting Israel as late as March 31, amid threats to US assets and Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Traders weighed verified IRGC-claimed actions against de-escalation signals, with no major diplomatic breakthroughs by the deadline; ongoing strikes into early April underscore persistent risks of further retaliation or ground operations.

title: 2026 NCAA Tournament Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament.

If it is impossible for a team to win the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament according to the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated) , the corresponding market will immediately resolve to “No”.

If for any reason no winner is declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NCAA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michigan Wolverines secured the 2026 NCAA men's basketball national championship with a 69-63 victory over UConn Huskies in the title game on April 6 at Lucas Oil Stadium, ending a 37-year drought since their 1989 triumph. Their dominant March Madness run featured a 91-73 Final Four rout of No. 1 Arizona, showcasing elite defense, balanced scoring, and coach Dusty May's tactical edge through five games averaging over 90 points scored. Traders' 100% consensus on Michigan reflects this conclusive outcome, with UConn's 0.1% residual pricing negligible post-resolution; no realistic challenges remain absent extraordinary disputes over eligibility or officiating, which official NCAA verification has affirmed.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: US and Israeli airstrikes have targeted Iranian nuclear sites, missile facilities, petrochemical plants, and leadership—including the recent killing of the IRGC intelligence chief in Tehran—severely degrading Tehran's retaliatory capabilities since the conflict erupted on February 28, 2026. Iran persists with near-daily missile barrages on Israel, downing US warplanes and rejecting ceasefire proposals amid President Trump's demands for Strait of Hormuz access and regime concessions. Diplomatic efforts, including indirect talks, remain stalled with no breakthroughs in the past week, fueling trader consensus for a prolonged standoff as both sides signal further escalation absent major de-escalation signals. Upcoming negotiations or intensified strikes could shift dynamics before year-end resolution deadlines.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) for third-most seats in Colombia's Chamber of Representatives following the March 8, 2026 legislative elections, where preliminary tallies with over 99% of mesas escrutadas show Pacto Histórico securing the largest bloc, ahead of Centro Democrático in second. PLC's consistent edge stems from strong departmental performances, outpacing Partido Conservador Colombiano and others like Alianza Verde or Partido de la U, as confirmed in late-March scrutineering completions by Registraduría Nacional. Final certification awaits, but no major recounts or disputes have emerged to threaten this order. Upsets could arise from special peace curules reallocations or legal challenges in tight districts, though historical patterns suggest stability post-preconteo.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Real Madrid CF and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will Real Madrid CF win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Real Madrid CF wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.groupItemTitle: Draw (Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München)

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich holds a slim trader consensus edge at 41.5% implied probability for the Champions League quarter-final first leg at the Bernabéu, driven by Harry Kane's confirmed availability after an ankle injury scare during international duty, bolstering their attack against a depleted Real Madrid side missing Thibaut Courtois and Rodrygo through injury, with Ferland Mendy doubtful. Real's 35.5% reflects home advantage and recent UCL dominance—winless Bayern in their last nine meetings (D2 L7)—but six stars including Mbappé, Vinícius Júnior, Bellingham and Tchouaméni risk yellow-card suspension for the second leg, fostering caution. The 23.5% draw pricing underscores evenly matched form, Bundesliga leaders Bayern's momentum and La Liga frontrunners Real's resilience despite rotation fatigue.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal tops trader consensus at 25.5% implied probability to win the UEFA Champions League, buoyed by their perfect 8-0-0 league phase record and a 3-1 aggregate round of 16 victory over Bayer Leverkusen, positioning them favorably against Sporting CP in the quarterfinal first leg today. Bayern Munich trails closely at 22.5% after a dominant 10-2 aggregate thrashing of Atalanta, though their blockbuster clash with Real Madrid introduces uncertainty. Barcelona (16.5%) advanced past Newcastle 8-3 on aggregate ahead of a tactical Spanish derby versus Atletico Madrid, while PSG (15.5%) demolished Chelsea 8-3 but faces high-pressing Liverpool. These heavyweight quarterfinal ties and knockout unpredictability keep the race tightly contested among Europe's elite.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a razor-thin contest for Brazil's October 4, 2026, presidential first-round vote, with incumbent Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva holding a slim 41.5% implied probability over Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's 38.3%, mirroring recent polls like Datafolha (March 7) and Reuters-reported surveys (March 25–30) showing technical ties in simulated runoffs. Lula's edge stems from incumbency advantages, rising approval amid economic stabilization, and his March 31 confirmation of Geraldo Alckmin as running mate, while Flávio Bolsonaro has surged on his father's endorsement and right-wing consolidation against the PT. The race stays tight due to polarized voting blocs and low support for alternatives like Renan Santos (6.7%); separation could arise from upcoming debates, economic data, or scandals ahead of a potential October 25 runoff.

title: Who will be confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next individual formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve.

Formal confirmation as Chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm a nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump formally nominated former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh as the next Fed Chair in late January 2026, with the nomination transmitted to the Senate on March 4 ahead of Jerome Powell's term expiration on May 15. Trader consensus reflects strong momentum as the Senate Banking Committee scheduled Warsh's confirmation hearing for April 16, signaling smooth procedural progress amid reports of broad acclaim from key Republicans. Warsh's prior Fed experience and views on inflation position him as a consensus pick in a Republican-controlled Senate. Challenges could arise from unexpected opposition in hearings, a withdrawn nomination, or late-breaking scandals, though Powell has pledged to remain until a successor is confirmed.

title: Next President of Vietnam
description:
Vietnam’s President is selected through internal decisions of the Communist Party of Vietnam and its National Assembly. The Party is holding its 14th National Congress from January 19 to January 25, 2026, after which the National Assembly of Vietnam is expected to elect leadership positions including President.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is formally elected by the National Assembly to assume the office of President of Vietnam.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected to assume the office of President. Any acting, interim, or caretaker President will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If the offices of President of Vietnam and General Secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam are merged such that an individual is elected to hold a joint office as leader of the party and head of state of Vietnam, this market will resolve to that individual.

If no such President is elected by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the National Assembly of Vietnam; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vietnam's National Assembly unanimously elected Communist Party General Secretary Tô Lâm as state president for the 2026-2031 term on April 7, 2026, during the first session of the 16th legislature, following the ruling party's near-total sweep of March parliamentary elections. This rare consolidation of the top party and state roles—breaking from the traditional "four pillars" separation—reflects Tô Lâm's entrenched dominance after his reappointment as general secretary at the 14th National Party Congress in January, driving trader consensus to imply virtual certainty at 100%. While procedural formalities like the oath-taking proceed without hitches, extraordinary challenges such as health issues, internal party disputes, or unprecedented legal reversals could theoretically shift the outcome before final resolution.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US airstrikes alongside Israel have severely degraded Iran's air force, navy, and missile capabilities since late February 2026, eliminating key leaders and over 5,000 targets in six weeks of operations, prompting President Trump to signal an imminent drawdown. In the past 48 hours, Trump held a news conference after a successful rescue of a downed US airman in Iran, reiterated a 2-3 week timeline for ending military actions, and threatened strikes on infrastructure like the Strait of Hormuz unless reopened by Tuesday's deadline. Negotiations continue amid rejected ceasefire proposals, with Pentagon officials noting dwindling high-value targets and no formal end announcement yet, as ground operations remain under consideration.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: FC Bayern München (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Bayern München" if FC Bayern München win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Madrid CF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Madrid and Bayern Munich head into their UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at the Bernabéu amid injury crises tilting trader sentiment toward a competitive tie. Madrid's Thibaut Courtois is ruled out for both legs with a thigh injury, forcing Andriy Lunin into goal, while Rodrygo remains sidelined long-term by an ACL tear and Éder Militão nears return from a muscle issue around April 1. Bayern faces a goalkeeper nightmare with Manuel Neuer (calf), Sven Ulreich (adductor), and Jonas Urbig (concussion) unavailable, plus Jamal Musiala out with a fibula injury. Madrid's home advantage and recent La Liga momentum counter Bayern's strong aggregate win over Atalanta, but vulnerabilities on both benches heighten upset potential.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export terminal handling 90% of its crude shipments in the Persian Gulf, remains fully under Iranian armed forces control despite U.S. precision airstrikes on March 14, 2026, that targeted over 90 military sites while sparing infrastructure. President Trump has threatened ground seizure to disrupt Tehran's energy revenue amid escalating U.S.-Iran conflict, but Iranian reinforcements—including additional troops, air defenses, traps, and mines—have fortified the island since late March. Recent Iranian reports confirm uninterrupted and even increased oil exports as of April 4. Traders monitor potential U.S. invasion risks and diplomatic signals, with no confirmed loss of control to date.
